hero.description: Your official information hub for exhibiting at GCAxTGS 2026. All schedules, rules, booth specs, and contacts — in one place.
hero.descriptionTh: ศูนย์รวมข้อมูลสำคัญสำหรับผู้จัดแสดงงาน GCAxTGS 2026

infoGrid.title: Everything you need
infoGrid.subtitle: All essential information for your participation at QSNCC, Hall 2–4.
infoGrid.subtitleTh: ข้อมูลสำคัญทุกอย่างสำหรับการเข้าร่วมแสดงสินค้าที่ QSNCC

generalInfo.desc: Event overview, dates, venue details, organiser contacts, and downloadable exhibitor manual.

schedule.desc: Setup, show day hours, non-sound periods, closing ceremony, and teardown schedule.

venue.desc: QSNCC hall specifications, loading zones, parking, elevator capacity, and floor plans.

rules.desc: Booth height limits, sound control policy, safety requirements, and contractor guidelines.

rawSpace.desc: Construction limits, engineer certification requirements, electrical, and utility order forms.

shellScheme.desc: Fascia name, furniture, graphics printing, electricity, and included booth specifications.

freight.desc: Loading dock access, vehicle limits, move-in timelines, and freight service order forms.

travel.desc: Recommended hotels, transportation from airport, and hotel reservation links.

visa.desc: Invitation letter requests and supporting documentation for international exhibitors.

schedule.title: Exhibition Schedule
schedule.subtitle: October 17–19, 2026 at Queen Sirikit National Convention Center
schedule.subtitleTh: ศูนย์การประชุมแห่งชาติสิริกิติ์ ห้อง Hall 2–4 ชั้น G

notice.label: Important Notice
notice.title: Non-Sound Period
notice.desc: On October 17 only, all exhibitors must turn off sound from 11:00 to 12:30. This coincides with the opening ceremony.

setup.title: Build-up & Setup Period
setup.detail: 08:00–24:00 both days · Booth construction and installation · 18 loading lanes for 6-wheel vehicles · Do NOT move exhibits on Oct 15 — allowed Oct 16 only

day1.title: Show Day 1 — Opening
day1.detail: Fast lane (Exhibitor): 09:45 · Public: 10:00–19:30 · Non-sound period: 11:00–12:30 · Ceiling lighting off 100% during this window

day3.title: Show Day 3 — Closing & Teardown
day3.detail: Show: 10:00–19:30 · Closing ceremony: 18:00–19:00 · Hall closes: 19:00 · All teardown starts: 20:00–24:00

contacts.title: Who to call
contacts.subtitle: ผู้ที่ควรติดต่อสำหรับการดำเนินงานต่างๆ

emergency.label: On-site Emergency
emergency.title: QSNCC Emergency Services
emergency.desc: Available during setup and all show days

generalInfo.title: General Information
generalInfo.titleTh: ข้อมูลทั่วไป
generalInfo.subtitle: Everything you need to know before the show

overview.title: GCAxTGS 2026
overview.desc: Gamescom Asia × Thailand Game Show 2026 is Southeast Asia's largest gaming exhibition, co-organized by Koelnmesse and True Digital Group. The event brings together game publishers, hardware brands, esports teams, and gaming communities under one roof at QSNCC, Bangkok.
overview.descTh: งานเกมส์ระดับนานาชาติที่ใหญ่ที่สุดในเอเชียตะวันออกเฉียงใต้

keyDates.title: Important Timeline
keyDates.subtitle: Mark these dates in your calendar

formDeadline.title: Booth Design Submission (T2A)
formDeadline.desc: Submit floor plan and 3D render to organiser

setup.title: Build-up & Setup
setup.desc: 08:00–24:00 both days. All booths must be completed by Oct 16, 24:00.

teardown.title: Teardown
teardown.desc: Starts at 20:00 after hall closes. All exhibits must be cleared by 24:00.

parking.label: Parking
parking.value: Available at QSNCC, basement B1–B3. Exhibitor rate applies.

bts.label: BTS / MRT
bts.value: MRT Queen Sirikit National Convention Center (exit 1–3, direct connection)

loading.label: Loading Docks
loading.value: 18 lanes for 6-wheel vehicles. See freight section for access schedule.

elevator.label: Freight Elevator
elevator.value: Capacity 5,000 kg. Available during setup and teardown only.

koelnmesse.name: Koelnmesse GmbH
koelnmesse.desc: World's leading trade fair company, host of Gamescom in Cologne. Provides brand, international exhibitor network, and event expertise.

trueDigital.name: True Digital Group
trueDigital.role: Local Organizer · Thailand Game Show License
trueDigital.desc: Thailand's leading digital platform and event organizer. Manages local operations, marketing, and exhibitor services.

blackdog.name: Blackdog Organizer
blackdog.desc: Official booth construction contractor. Handles shell scheme, raw space build-out, and on-site technical support.

downloads.title: Exhibitor Manual & Documents
downloads.subtitle: All essential documents for your participation

manual.title: Exhibitor Manual 2026
manual.desc: Complete guide covering all rules, schedules, forms, and contacts

floorPlan.title: Exhibition Floor Plan
floorPlan.desc: Hall 2–4 layout with booth locations and service points

formPack.title: Form Pack (All Forms)
formPack.desc: M1–M7 and T1–T12 blank forms in one package

downloads.note: Documents are updated periodically. Last updated: April 2026.

rules.title: Rules & Regulations
rules.subtitle: Venue policies, conduct requirements, and compliance guidelines

general.title: Exhibitor Conduct
general.subtitle: Applies to all exhibitors, staff, and contractors throughout the event

badge.label: Badges Required
badge.value: All exhibitors and staff must wear their official event badge at all times inside the venue. Badges are non-transferable.

access.label: Access Hours
access.value: Exhibitors may access the hall from 09:45 on show days. The hall is not accessible outside scheduled hours without prior approval.

conduct.label: Professional Conduct
conduct.value: Exhibitors must maintain a professional and respectful environment. Harassment, offensive displays, or disruptive behaviour will result in removal.

smoking.label: No Smoking
smoking.value: Smoking is strictly prohibited inside all venue buildings. Designated smoking areas are outside the main entrance only.

food.label: Food & Beverages
food.value: F&B distribution from booths must be pre-approved. All F&B must be ordered through official venue services (Form T12).

media.label: Photography & Media
media.value: Commercial photography or filming must be pre-arranged with the organiser. Media passes are required for press activities.

children.label: Minors
children.value: Children under 15 must be accompanied by an adult at all times. No unsupervised minors in build-up or teardown areas.

booth.title: Booth & Display
booth.subtitle: Rules governing booth design, content, and operation

boundaries.label: Booth Boundaries
boundaries.value: All display materials, furniture, and activities must stay within contracted booth boundaries. No encroachment into aisles.

signage.label: Signage Height
signage.value: Shell scheme: max 2.5 m. Raw space: max 6 m with engineer cert. No signs may protrude beyond the contracted booth area.

projection.label: Screen Projection
projection.value: Rear-projection systems must be contained within booth boundaries. Forward projection affecting neighbouring booths is prohibited.

demos.label: Live Demonstrations
demos.value: Demonstrations attracting crowds must not block aisles. Crowd management is the exhibitor's responsibility.

giveaways.label: Giveaways & Sampling
giveaways.value: Distribution of samples or gifts must take place within the booth only. No distribution in aisles or common areas.

subletting.label: Subletting
subletting.value: Booth space may not be sublet, shared, or transferred to any third party without written organiser consent.

sound.title: Audio & Volume Control
sound.subtitle: Violations may result in immediate shutdown of your audio system
sound.note: Sound levels must not disturb neighbouring booths. QSNCC staff will issue one warning before mandating shutdown of non-compliant audio systems.

level.label: Max Volume
level.value: Audio must not exceed 85 dB at the booth boundary. Organisers may require volume reduction at any time.

nonSound.label: Non-Sound Period
nonSound.value: October 17, 11:00–12:30 only. All audio must be OFF during the opening ceremony. Ceiling lights also off 100%.

music.label: Licensed Music
music.value: All music played must be legally licensed. Exhibitors are responsible for any copyright or licensing fees.

screens.label: Video Screens
screens.value: Screens facing the aisle must display content appropriate for all public audiences at all times.

overtime.label: Overtime Rate
overtime.value: Extended access beyond scheduled hours: 5,000 THB per booth per hour, subject to availability.

safety.title: Health & Safety
safety.subtitle: Non-compliance may result in immediate booth closure

emergency.label: Emergency Exits
emergency.value: Emergency exit routes must remain clear and unobstructed at all times. No structures or materials in evacuation corridors.

fire.label: Fire Safety
fire.value: All materials must be fire-retardant certified. No open flames, candles, or high-heat devices without prior written approval.

electrical.label: Electrical Safety
electrical.value: No overloading of circuits. All electrical work by licensed electricians only. Faulty wiring will be shut down immediately.

flooring.label: Trip Hazards
flooring.value: All cables must be covered or secured. Raised flooring must have ramped edges on all open sides.

weight.label: Floor Loading
weight.value: Maximum floor loading: 500 kg/m². Heavy equipment must be distributed. Check with QSNCC operations for exceptions.

firstaid.label: First Aid
firstaid.value: QSNCC medical team is on call during all show days. Medical emergency: 02-229-3001. Security: 02-229-3000.

contractor.title: Contractor Guidelines
contractor.subtitle: All contractors must comply with venue and organiser policies

registration.label: Registration
registration.value: All contractors must register via Form T2 at least 2 weeks before build-up. Unregistered personnel will be denied entry.

insurance.label: Insurance
insurance.value: Valid public liability insurance (min 5M THB) required. Certificate must be submitted with Form T2.

hours.label: Working Hours
hours.value: Contractors permitted during build-up (08:00–24:00) and teardown (from 20:00 on Oct 19) only.

conduct.label: Conduct
conduct.value: All contractor staff must wear venue-issued contractor badges. Misconduct will result in immediate removal from site.

damage.label: Damage & Liability
damage.value: Exhibitors are responsible for any damage caused by their contractors. Repair costs will be charged to the exhibitor.

inspection.label: Inspection Rights
inspection.value: QSNCC and organiser staff reserve the right to inspect any booth structure during build-up at any time.

contactsList.title: List of Contacts
contactsList.subtitle: Key contacts for all operational matters during GCAxTGS 2026

emergency.title: QSNCC Emergency Services
emergency.desc: Available during all build-up, show, and teardown periods

key.title: Who to Call
key.subtitle: Primary contacts for each area of responsibility

parin.name: Parin Wilaijit
parin.topics: General enquiries, booth assignments, badges, forms

wichittra.name: Wichittra Phadungsri
wichittra.topics: Shell scheme, raw space build, furniture, graphics

suttipat.name: Suttipat Neamkhuntod
suttipat.topics: Electrical orders, freight elevator, venue operations

operations.label: Hall Operations
operations.desc: Loading docks, freight elevator, hall access

security.label: Security Office
security.desc: Badge issues, lost & found, venue security

rawSpace.title: Raw Space Booth
rawSpace.subtitle: Construction rules, certification, and technical requirements

overview.title: Custom Booth Build
overview.desc: Raw space booths give exhibitors full creative freedom to design and construct a custom booth. All construction must comply with QSNCC venue regulations and GCAxTGS guidelines. An approved contractor and certified engineer are mandatory.

construction.title: Build Requirements
construction.subtitle: All structural work must comply with QSNCC fire and safety standards
construction.note: Any booth exceeding 2.5 m in height requires submission of structural drawings signed by a licensed structural engineer before build-up begins.

height.label: Maximum Height
height.value: 6 metres from hall floor. Structures above 2.5 m require engineer sign-off.

setback.label: Aisle Setback
setback.value: Minimum 0.5 m setback from all aisle boundaries on open sides.

materials.label: Permitted Materials
materials.value: All materials must be fire-retardant certified. No combustible cladding. Timber must be treated or covered.

flooring.label: Flooring
flooring.value: Raised flooring allowed up to 15 cm. Must have ramp access on all open sides. No nailing or drilling into hall floor.

rigging.label: Overhead Rigging
rigging.value: No rigging from hall ceiling. All suspended elements must be self-supporting within the booth footprint.

walls.label: Shared Walls
walls.value: Walls on neighbouring booth boundaries must be finished on both sides (white paint minimum).

lighting.label: Internal Lighting
lighting.value: LED only. No open flame, high-heat lamps, or exposed wiring. All wiring must be enclosed in conduit.

storage.label: Storage & Empties
storage.value: All empty crates and packing materials must be removed from the hall during show hours. On-site storage is not provided.

engineer.title: Structural Certification
engineer.subtitle: Mandatory for all raw space booths above 2.5 m
engineer.note: Certification documents must be submitted to the organiser via Form T2A no later than September 15. Construction will not be permitted to begin without approval.

design.title: Submit Booth Design
design.desc: Submit floor plan, elevation drawings, and 3D render via Form T2A by Sep 15.

approval.title: Organiser Approval
approval.desc: Organiser reviews and approves design within 5 business days. Amendments may be requested.

cert.title: Engineer Signs Structural Doc
cert.desc: Licensed structural engineer must sign and stamp all structural drawings for booths above 2.5 m.

submit.title: Submit Cert to QSNCC
submit.desc: Certified drawings submitted to QSNCC venue operations team prior to build-up.

onsite.title: On-site Inspection
onsite.desc: QSNCC reserves the right to inspect any booth during build-up. Non-compliant structures must be modified.

electrical.title: Power & Lighting
electrical.subtitle: All electrical work must be performed by a licensed electrician
electrical.note: Electrical order Form T5 must be submitted to QSNCC by October 1. Late orders may not be fulfilled.

supply.label: Power Supply
supply.value: 220V / 50Hz single phase or 380V / 50Hz three phase. Voltage fluctuation ±10%.

order.label: Order Form
order.value: Form T5 — Electricity & Lighting. Submit to QSNCC by October 1.

included.label: What's Included
included.value: Power connection point only. Booth distributor, wiring, and fixtures are exhibitor's responsibility.

electrician.label: Licensed Electrician
electrician.value: All internal wiring must be installed by a licensed electrician. QSNCC can provide referrals.

generator.label: Generators
generator.value: No private generators permitted inside the hall. Additional power must be ordered from QSNCC.

overnight.label: Overnight Power
overnight.value: Power is cut at 24:00 during build-up. Additional overnight power must be pre-arranged with QSNCC.

contractor.title: Nominated Stand Contractor
contractor.subtitle: All contractors must be registered before accessing the venue
contractor.note: Exhibitors using their own nominated contractor must complete Form T2 and submit contractor details to the organiser at least 2 weeks before build-up.

form.label: Registration Form
form.value: Form T2 — Nominated Stand Contractor. Submit contractor name, license number, and insurance certificate.

insurance.label: Insurance
insurance.value: Contractor must hold valid public liability insurance (minimum 5M THB). Certificate required at registration.

badge.label: Contractor Badges
badge.value: All contractor staff must wear venue-issued contractor badges at all times during build-up and teardown.

blackdog.label: Official Contractor
blackdog.value: Blackdog Organizer is the official stand contractor and can handle all raw space construction. Contact for quote.

shellScheme.title: Shell Scheme Booth
shellScheme.subtitle: Standard booth specifications, inclusions, and add-on services

overview.title: Standard Booth Package
overview.desc: Shell scheme booths are pre-built standard booths provided by the official contractor Blackdog Organizer. The package includes walls, flooring, fascia nameplate, basic furniture, and electrical connection.

included.title: Standard Package
included.subtitle: Every shell scheme booth includes the following at no extra charge

walls.label: Octanorm Walls
walls.value: White Octanorm system walls, 2.5 m height. Three closed sides (open to aisle).

carpet.label: Carpet Flooring
carpet.value: Grey carpet tiles covering the full booth footprint.

fascia.label: Fascia Nameplate
fascia.value: 1-line company name on standard fascia board. Submitted via Form T1.

power.label: Electrical Supply
power.value: 500W power socket included. Additional power available via Form T5.

lighting.label: Overhead Lighting
lighting.value: 2× spotlight (100W equivalent) per 9 sqm. Additional spotlights available.

table.label: Table & Chairs
table.value: 1× folding table, 2× folding chairs included per booth unit.

maxDeco.label: Max Decoration Height
maxDeco.value: 2.5 m — nothing may protrude above wall top

drilling.label: Drilling / Nailing
drilling.value: Strictly prohibited on Octanorm panels. Use velcro or panel clips only.

openSides.label: Open Sides
openSides.value: Aisle-facing sides are open. Additional walling can be ordered.

flooring.label: Flooring
flooring.value: Carpet tiles. Raised flooring or other finishes not permitted without prior approval.

fascia.title: Form T1 — Fascia Name
fascia.subtitle: Submit your company name for the fascia board
fascia.desc: The fascia board displays your company name at the top of the booth on aisle-facing sides. Submit the exact name via Form T1. Standard font and colour apply; custom fonts are not available.

length.label: Character Limit
length.value: Maximum 30 characters per line including spaces.

language.label: Language
language.value: English or Thai. Mixed language on single line is permitted.

font.label: Font & Colour
font.value: Standard venue font, white text on black background. Custom colours not available.

deadline.label: Submission Deadline
deadline.value: Form T1 must be submitted by September 30 to guarantee inclusion.

changes.label: Changes
changes.value: Changes after submission may incur a 500 THB revision fee and are subject to availability.

addons.title: Optional Upgrades
addons.subtitle: Order through Blackdog Organizer. Deadline: October 1.

power.label: Additional Power
power.desc: Extra sockets or 3-phase supply. Order via Form T5.

lighting.label: Extra Spotlights
lighting.desc: Additional 100W LED spotlights. Mounting on Octanorm rail.

furniture.label: Furniture Upgrade
furniture.desc: Counters, display pedestals, lockable cabinets, bar stools.

graphics.label: Panel Graphics
graphics.desc: Printed graphic panels installed on Octanorm walls. Artwork required.

av.label: AV Equipment
av.desc: LCD screens, TV mounts, PA systems, and cabling.

walling.label: Extra Walling
walling.desc: Additional Octanorm panels to close open aisle sides.

paint.label: No Painting
paint.value: Octanorm panels must not be painted or permanently marked. Damage will be charged to the exhibitor.

drilling.label: No Drilling
drilling.value: No drilling, nailing, screwing, or stapling into walls, floor, or ceiling. Velcro and panel clips only.

height.label: Height Limit
height.value: Decorations, banners, and displays must not exceed 2.5 m or protrude above wall top.

storage.label: Storage
storage.value: No storage behind back wall or in aisles. All empty boxes must be removed from hall during show hours.

subletting.label: No Subletting
subletting.value: Booth space may not be sublet, shared, or assigned to a third party without written organiser consent.

soundSchedule.title: Sound Schedule
soundSchedule.subtitle: Volume rules, non-sound period, and audio compliance guidelines

overview.title: Sound Schedule Overview
overview.desc: Sound is permitted during all show hours except the mandatory non-sound window on opening day (October 17, 11:00–12:30). All booths must comply with the 85 dB limit at the booth boundary.

nonSound.title: Non-Sound Period
nonSound.subtitle: October 17 (Opening day) only — 11:00 to 12:30
nonSound.desc: On October 17 only, all exhibitors must immediately silence all audio equipment for the duration of the opening ceremony. This includes all music, video sound, announcements, and demonstrations.

off.title: What must be turned off
off.lighting: Hall ceiling lights will be turned off 100%

nonSound.reason: This window coincides with the official GCAxTGS 2026 opening ceremony. Organiser and venue staff will enforce compliance. One warning will be issued — second violation results in audio shutdown for the remainder of the show day.
nonSound.warning: Failure to comply will result in immediate shutdown of your booth audio system by QSNCC staff.

daily.title: Daily Sound Hours
daily.subtitle: Sound permitted during all public show hours except the Oct 17 non-sound window

rules.title: Sound Rules
rules.subtitle: Violations may result in immediate shutdown of your audio system

volume.label: Maximum Volume
volume.value: 85 dB measured at the booth boundary. QSNCC staff may check at any time.

music.label: Licensed Music Required
music.value: All music must be legally licensed. Exhibitors bear full responsibility for copyright and licensing fees.

subwoofer.label: Subwoofer / Bass
subwoofer.value: Low-frequency bass must not be audible or disruptive beyond adjacent booths.

screens.label: AV Screen Orientation
screens.value: Screens and speakers must face inward. Forward projection affecting neighbours is prohibited.

organiser.label: Organiser Authority
organiser.value: Organiser staff may order volume reduction or shutdown at any time without prior notice.

rate.label: Overtime Rate
rate.value: 5,000 THB per booth per hour for access beyond scheduled show hours, subject to availability.

first.label: First Violation
first.value: Written warning issued by organiser or QSNCC staff.

second.label: Second Violation
second.value: Immediate audio system shutdown for the remainder of the show day.

repeat.label: Repeat Offence
repeat.value: May result in permanent audio restriction for the duration of the event.

venueInfo.title: Venue Information
venueInfo.subtitle: QSNCC hall specs, loading, parking, and directions

overview.title: Queen Sirikit National Convention Center
overview.addressTh: ศูนย์การประชุมแห่งชาติสิริกิติ์ ถ.รัชดาภิเษก คลองเตย กรุงเทพฯ
overview.copyLabel: Copy Thai address for taxi driver

halls.title: Exhibition Halls
halls.subtitle: Hall 2, Hall 3, and Hall 4 — Ground Floor (Zone G)

ceiling.label: Ceiling Height
ceiling.value: Approximately 10 m clear height throughout Hall 2–4.

columns.label: Column-Free Spans
columns.value: All halls feature wide column-free spans for flexible booth layouts.

floor.label: Floor Loading
floor.value: 500 kg/m² maximum floor loading across all hall zones.

power.label: Power Grid Points
power.value: Electrical grid access points every 9 sqm throughout the halls.

connected.label: Hall Connectivity
connected.value: Hall 2, 3, and 4 are fully interconnected on the ground floor.

loading.title: Loading & Freight
loading.subtitle: Ground floor service road — 18 loading dock lanes

lanes.label: Loading Dock Lanes
lanes.value: 18 lanes on the ground floor service road. Open during build-up 08:00–24:00.

vehicles.label: Vehicle Limit
vehicles.value: 6-wheel vehicles maximum. No larger trucks permitted inside the service road area.

elevator.label: Freight Elevator
elevator.value: 5,000 kg capacity. Approximate dimensions: 4 m × 6 m. Schedule in advance with QSNCC.

showHours.label: Show Hours Policy
showHours.value: No private vehicles permitted during public show hours. Loading access during build-up and teardown only.

parking.title: Parking
parking.subtitle: Basement B1–B3 parking available for exhibitors

location.label: Location
location.value: Basement levels B1, B2, and B3 beneath the main QSNCC building.

entry.label: Entry Point
entry.value: Gate 1, Ratchadaphisek Road entrance. Follow venue signage.

rate.label: Exhibitor Rate
rate.value: Daily exhibitor parking rate available. Validate at the exhibitor registration desk.

height.label: Height Limit
height.value: Maximum vehicle height 2.1 m for basement entry.

access.title: Getting Here
access.subtitle: MRT direct connection, taxi, and other transport options

mrt.title: MRT (Blue Line)
mrt.value: Station: Queen Sirikit National Convention Center. Exits 1, 2, and 3 have direct covered walkway connection to the hall. This is the fastest and most reliable option.

taxi.title: Taxi / Grab
taxi.value: From Sukhumvit approx 15 min. From Suvarnabhumi Airport (BKK) approx 40 min. Use the Thai address below for taxi drivers.

thaiAddress.label: Thai address for taxi driver
thaiAddress.value: ศูนย์การประชุมแห่งชาติสิริกิติ์ ถ.รัชดาภิเษก คลองเตย กรุงเทพฯ

travel.title: Travel & Accommodation
travel.subtitle: Getting to Bangkok, reaching QSNCC, and recommended hotels

transport.title: Getting to Bangkok
transport.subtitle: Arriving from Suvarnabhumi or Don Mueang Airport

bkk.title: Suvarnabhumi Airport (BKK)
bkk.rail: Airport Rail Link to Makkasan then MRT transfer to Queen Sirikit NCC station. Total approx 60 min.
bkk.taxi: Taxi direct to QSNCC approx 45 min · estimated 350–500 THB depending on traffic.

dmk.title: Don Mueang Airport (DMK)
dmk.taxi: Taxi to QSNCC approx 60 min depending on traffic. Grab app recommended for transparent pricing.

transport.grab: Grab app (ride-hailing) is recommended for all local transport. Available from both airports and throughout the city.

toVenue.title: Getting to QSNCC
toVenue.subtitle: Multiple options for reaching the venue from around Bangkok

mrt.title: MRT Blue Line
mrt.value: Station: Queen Sirikit National Convention Center. Exits 1–3 have a direct covered walkway into the hall. No weather exposure.

bts.title: BTS Skytrain
bts.value: Transfer at Asok/Sukhumvit station to MRT Sukhumvit, then ride 2 stops to Queen Sirikit NCC.

taxi.title: Taxi / Grab
taxi.value: Recommended drop-off: Gate 1, Ratchadaphisek Rd entrance. Note: no ride-hailing pickup/drop-off at service areas during build-up days.

hotels.title: Recommended Hotels
hotels.subtitle: Nearby accommodation options — GCAxTGS does not have an official room block. Book directly with hotels.

novotel.name: Novotel Bangkok Fenix Silom
novotel.desc: 4-star business hotel near Silom / Chong Nonsi BTS

marriottQP.name: Marriott Marquis Queen's Park
marriottQP.desc: Luxury 5-star hotel, close to Phrom Phong BTS

grande.name: Grande Centre Point Hotel Terminal 21
grande.desc: 4-star with direct connection to Terminal 21 mall

sukhumvit.name: Sukhumvit Park Bangkok Marriott Executive Apartments
sukhumvit.desc: Serviced apartments — ideal for multi-night stays

tips.title: Travel Tips
tips.subtitle: Practical advice for getting around Bangkok during the show

transit.label: BTS / MRT Peak Hours
transit.value: Avoid 08:00–09:30 and 17:30–19:30. Trains are extremely crowded during rush hours.

grab.label: Grab App
grab.value: Grab is the most reliable ride-hailing option. Set pick-up/drop-off carefully around the venue.

weather.label: October Weather
weather.value: Hot and humid — 28–34°C. Occasional rain showers. Bring an umbrella or pack a light rain jacket.

currency.label: Currency
currency.value: Thai Baht (THB). ATMs widely available. Major cards accepted at hotels; some local vendors prefer cash.

tipping.label: Tipping
tipping.value: Not mandatory in Thailand, but appreciated. 20–50 THB for taxis, 10% at restaurants if no service charge.

convenience.label: 7-Eleven & Pharmacies
convenience.value: 7-Eleven and pharmacy outlets are available inside the QSNCC building.

freight.title: Freight & Exhibits
freight.subtitle: Loading schedule, vehicle rules, move-in procedures, and prohibited items

overview.title: Logistics Overview
overview.desc: All exhibit and freight movement in and out of the hall must comply with QSNCC loading dock procedures. Read this section carefully before build-up — non-compliance may delay your setup.

schedule.title: Access by Day
schedule.subtitle: Loading dock access hours differ by phase — plan deliveries accordingly

oct15.title: Build-up Day 1
oct15.detail: Booth construction materials only. No exhibit products or merchandise. 18 loading lanes open.
oct15.warning: DO NOT bring exhibit products or merchandise on Oct 15.

oct16.title: Build-up Day 2 — Exhibit Move-in
oct16.detail: All construction AND exhibit products allowed. Priority loading for final setup. All exhibits must be in place by 24:00.

oct17.title: Show Days
oct17.detail: Loading dock closed during public show hours. Emergency resupply only, subject to QSNCC operations approval. No vehicles permitted.

oct19.title: Teardown & Move-out
oct19.detail: All 18 loading lanes reopen from 20:00. All exhibits and booth materials must be removed by 24:00.

vehicles.title: Loading Dock & Vehicle Policy
vehicles.subtitle: Strictly enforced — non-compliant vehicles will be turned away
vehicles.note: All vehicles must check in at the loading dock registration point before entering. Unregistered vehicles will not be permitted.

maxSize.label: Maximum Vehicle Size
maxSize.value: 6-wheel trucks maximum (up to 10 tonnes GVW). Larger vehicles (18-wheel, articulated lorries) are NOT permitted inside the hall compound.

lanes.label: Loading Lanes
lanes.value: 18 lanes available on the ground floor service road. Lanes are first-come first-served. No overnight parking in loading bays.

height.label: Overhead Clearance
height.value: Maximum vehicle height: 4.0 m for service road access. Check approach route — some overhead gantries may be lower.

checkin.label: Check-in Required
checkin.value: All vehicles must register at the loading dock office (Ground Floor, Service Road Gate). Valid exhibitor or contractor badge required.

time.label: Max Dwell Time
time.value: Maximum 2 hours per vehicle in loading bay. Vehicles left unattended beyond 2 hours may be removed at owner's cost.

hazmat.label: Hazardous Materials
hazmat.value: All hazardous goods (flammables, pressurised canisters, chemicals) require QSNCC Operations pre-approval. Submit manifest 7 days in advance.

elevator.title: Freight Elevator Access
elevator.subtitle: Pre-booking required during peak build-up hours
elevator.note: The freight elevator is available during build-up (Oct 15–16) and teardown (Oct 19 from 20:00) only. It is NOT available during show hours.

capacity.label: Weight Capacity
capacity.value: 5,000 kg per load. Do not overload — QSNCC staff will refuse entry if weight limit is exceeded.

dimensions.label: Car Dimensions
dimensions.value: Approximately 4.0 m (W) × 6.0 m (D) × 3.5 m (H). Verify with QSNCC operations for oversized items.

booking.label: How to Book
booking.value: Contact QSNCC Hall Operations (Suttipat Neamkhuntod, 02-229-3044) to schedule elevator slots during build-up. Walk-in use during off-peak hours only.

priority.label: Priority
priority.value: Booths with heavy machinery or oversized exhibits should book Oct 15 slots. Oct 16 morning is peak — expect queues.

staff.label: Operator
staff.value: QSNCC elevator operator must be present at all times. Exhibitors may not operate the elevator controls.

moveIn.title: Exhibit Handling Rules
moveIn.subtitle: Critical dates and procedures for all exhibit items

oct15ban.label: Oct 15 — No Exhibits
oct15ban.value: Exhibit products, merchandise, and display items must NOT be brought in on Oct 15. Construction materials only.

oct16ok.label: Oct 16 — Move-in Day
oct16ok.value: All exhibit items, products, and merchandise must be brought in and set up on Oct 16. This is the only permitted move-in day.

deadline.label: Setup Deadline
deadline.value: All exhibits must be fully set up and ready by Oct 16 at 24:00. No construction or major changes permitted after this time.

insurance.label: Exhibit Insurance
insurance.value: Exhibitors are strongly advised to insure all exhibit items. GCAxTGS and QSNCC accept no liability for lost, stolen, or damaged exhibits.

teardown.label: Teardown — Oct 19
teardown.value: Move-out begins at 20:00 on Oct 19. All items must be cleared by 24:00. Items left after 24:00 will be disposed at exhibitor's expense.

labels.label: Freight Labels
labels.value: All crates and packages should be clearly labelled with: Exhibitor name, booth number, hall, and contact phone number.

prohibited.title: What Cannot Be Brought In
prohibited.subtitle: Items found in violation will be removed immediately by QSNCC security

weapons.label: Weapons & Replicas
weapons.value: No real or replica firearms, bladed weapons, or any item that could be used as a weapon, unless pre-approved for display with deactivation certificate.

flammable.label: Flammable Liquids
flammable.value: No petrol, solvents, flammable sprays, or any Class 3 flammable liquids. Exceptions for small quantities of cleaning fluids (pre-approval required).

gas.label: Pressurised Gas
gas.value: No LPG, compressed gas cylinders, or pressurised canisters of any kind without explicit QSNCC Operations written approval.

animals.label: Live Animals
animals.value: No live animals of any kind without QSNCC management written approval and relevant government permits.

drones.label: Drones & UAVs
drones.value: No unmanned aerial vehicles inside the hall or on the venue premises without prior written approval from both QSNCC and the organiser.

food.label: Outside F&B for Resale
food.value: No outside food or beverages for sale or public distribution without organiser approval and valid F&B permit. Order through Form T12.

exhibitionSchedule.title: Exhibition Schedule
exhibitionSchedule.titleTh: กำหนดการจัดแสดงสินค้า
exhibitionSchedule.subtitle: Complete schedule for build-up, show days, and teardown

overview.title: 5-Day Event Overview
overview.subtitle: October 15–19, 2026 · Queen Sirikit National Convention Center

buildup.title: Setup & Installation
buildup.subtitle: October 15–16, 2026
buildup.note: All booth construction must be completed by Oct 16 at 24:00. Exhibitors who fail to complete by this deadline will be unable to make further changes.

access.label: Exhibitor Access
access.value: Exhibitor badge required. Fast lane available from 08:00.

vehicles.label: Loading Vehicles
vehicles.value: 18 lanes available for 6-wheel vehicles maximum. No larger trucks permitted inside the hall.

exhibits.label: Exhibit Move-In
exhibits.value: DO NOT move exhibits on Oct 15. Move-in of exhibits and products is allowed Oct 16 only.

elevator.label: Freight Elevator
elevator.value: Capacity 5,000 kg. Schedule with QSNCC operations team in advance.

contractor.label: Contractor Access
contractor.value: All contractors must register at main entrance. Unregistered contractors will be denied access.

day1.label: Show Day 1 — Opening
day1.special: Non-Sound Period: 11:00–12:30 · Ceiling lights off 100% during opening ceremony

teardown.title: Dismantle & Move-Out
teardown.subtitle: October 19, 2026 — starts 20:00
teardown.note: All exhibitors must clear their booths and remove all exhibits by 24:00 on October 19. Items left after the deadline will be disposed of at the exhibitor's expense.

deadline.label: Hard Deadline
deadline.value: 24:00 Oct 19 — all items must be removed

vehicles.label: Loading Vehicles
vehicles.value: 18 lanes available. Same vehicle restrictions as build-up.

contractor.label: Contractor Access
contractor.value: Contractors permitted from 20:00 with valid badge.

soundPolicy.title: Non-Sound Period
soundPolicy.subtitle: Opening ceremony, October 17 only
soundPolicy.desc: On October 17 (opening day) only, all exhibitors are required to turn off all audio, music, and video sound from 11:00 to 12:30. This window coincides with the official opening ceremony.

lighting.label: Ceiling lighting
lighting.value: Hall ceiling lights will be turned off 100%

overtime.label: Overtime rate
overtime.value: 5,000 THB per booth per hour for extended access beyond scheduled hours

applies.label: Applies to
applies.value: October 17 only — Show Day 2 and 3 are not affected

soundPolicy.warning: Failure to comply with the non-sound period will result in immediate shutdown of your booth's audio system by venue staff.

visa.title: Visa Application
visa.subtitle: Invitation letter service for international exhibitors requiring a Thai visa

overview.title: Official Invitation Letters
overview.desc: GCA x TGS 2026 provides official invitation letters to support visa applications for international exhibitors. Letters are issued by the organiser and may be submitted to the Royal Thai Embassy or consulate in your country.

eligibility.title: Eligibility for Invitation Letter
eligibility.subtitle: Only official exhibiting companies and their registered staff are eligible

who.label: Who Is Eligible
who.value: Registered exhibitors and staff listed in the official exhibitor registration form. Sub-let booth holders and unofficial participants are not eligible.

countries.label: Visa-Exempt Nationals
countries.value: Nationals of countries with visa-exempt agreements with Thailand do not require an invitation letter. Check the Royal Thai Embassy website for your country's status.

staff.label: Staff Per Company
staff.value: Up to 5 invitation letters per exhibiting company for booth staff. Additional letters for senior executives may be requested with justification.

buyers.label: Buyers & Visitors
buyers.value: Individual visitor visa applications are not handled by the organiser. Buyers and trade visitors must apply independently.

documents.title: What to Submit
documents.subtitle: Incomplete submissions will be returned and may miss the deadline

passport.label: Passport Copy
passport.value: Clear copy of bio-data page. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the event end date (April 19, 2027 minimum).

registration.label: Exhibitor Confirmation
registration.value: Copy of your signed booth contract or official exhibitor confirmation letter from the organiser.

form.label: Visa Request Form V01
form.value: Completed Form V01 — Visa Invitation Request. Download from the portal under Forms & Downloads. One form per person.

photo.label: Passport Photo
photo.value: One digital passport-size photo (white background, JPG, min. 300 dpi, taken within 6 months).

company.label: Company Letter
company.value: Official letter on company letterhead stating the applicant's name, title, purpose of visit, and authorised signature with company stamp.

process.title: How to Apply
process.subtitle: Submit all documents together — partial submissions will not be processed

s1.title: Download Form V01
s1.desc: Download the Visa Invitation Request form (Form V01) from the Forms & Downloads section of this portal.

s2.title: Prepare Documents
s2.desc: Gather all required documents: passport copy, photo, company letter, and exhibitor confirmation. Ensure the passport is valid until at least April 2027.

s3.title: Submit to Organiser
s3.desc: Email the complete package to visa@gcatgs.com with subject line: [VISA] Company Name – Applicant Name.

s4.title: Processing
s4.desc: The organiser reviews and issues the letter within 5–7 business days. Urgent requests (2 business days) are available for 500 THB per letter.

s5.title: Receive & Apply
s5.desc: You will receive a PDF invitation letter by email. Present this to the Royal Thai Embassy in your country along with your visa application.

timeline.title: Request Timeline
timeline.subtitle: Factor in embassy processing time — allow at least 4 weeks before your travel date

open.label: Requests Open
open.value: June 1, 2026 — Visa invitation letter requests open for all confirmed exhibitors.

deadline.label: Standard Deadline
deadline.value: August 31, 2026 — Last date for standard requests (5–7 business days processing).

late.label: Late Deadline
late.value: September 15, 2026 — Last date for urgent requests only (2 business days, 500 THB fee per letter).

cutoff.label: Hard Cutoff
cutoff.value: No requests accepted after September 15, 2026. Allow sufficient embassy processing time — we cannot guarantee visa approval.

noGuarantee.label: No Visa Guarantee
noGuarantee.value: An invitation letter supports your application but does not guarantee visa approval. The final decision rests with Thai immigration authorities.

cost.label: Letter Fee
cost.value: Standard invitation letters are free of charge. Urgent letters (within 2 business days) are 500 THB per letter.

responsibility.label: Applicant's Responsibility
responsibility.value: Applicants are responsible for meeting all visa requirements set by the Thai embassy in their country, including fees, interviews, and additional documents.

billing.title: Billing Setup
billing.subtitle: Invoice details, payment methods, and billing contact for all exhibitor fees

overview.title: Exhibitor Billing Overview
overview.desc: All exhibitor fees — booth rental, service orders, electricity, and contractor charges — are billed by the organiser (Gamer Company Association). Invoices are issued in Thai Baht. International exhibitors may pay by wire transfer.

invoiceInfo.title: Your Billing Information
invoiceInfo.subtitle: Submit accurate details to avoid invoice corrections and payment delays

name.label: Company Name (Legal)
name.value: Invoices will be issued in the legal company name as registered. Ensure the name matches your company registration document exactly.

address.label: Registered Address
address.value: Provide your full legal registered address including country. This will appear on the official tax invoice.

taxId.label: Tax ID / VAT Number
taxId.value: Thai companies must provide a 13-digit Tax Identification Number. Foreign companies provide their local VAT registration equivalent.

contact.label: Billing Contact
contact.value: Provide the name, email, and phone of the person responsible for approving and settling invoices.

form.label: Submit Via Form B01
form.value: Complete Form B01 — Billing Information Form and send to billing@gcatgs.com. Required before any invoice can be issued.

payment.title: Accepted Payment Methods
payment.subtitle: All payments must reference your exhibitor ID and invoice number

transfer.label: Bank Transfer (Thailand)
transfer.value: Kasikorn Bank (KBank) · Account: Gamer Company Association · Acct No: 001-1-23456-7 · Branch: Silom · Attach transfer slip to billing@gcatgs.com.

wire.label: International Wire Transfer
wire.value: SWIFT code and full banking details are shown on the invoice. International wire transfer fees are borne by the sender.

cheque.label: Cheque
cheque.value: Payable to "Gamer Company Association". Delivered to the organiser's office at least 3 business days before the due date. Post-dated cheques are not accepted.

cash.label: Cash (On-site Only)
cash.value: Cash payments in THB accepted at the organiser's on-site office (QSNCC Hall Office) during build-up and show days. Receipt issued.

tax.title: Tax Information
tax.subtitle: Thailand VAT and withholding tax rules apply to all transactions
tax.note: Exhibitors registered for VAT in Thailand may request a full tax invoice. Ensure your 13-digit Tax ID is submitted on Form B01 before August 1, 2026.

vat.label: VAT (7%)
vat.value: All fees are subject to 7% VAT, shown separately on the invoice. A full tax invoice is issued to registered VAT entities upon request.

wht.label: Withholding Tax (3%)
wht.value: Thai juristic persons may withhold 3% at source for service fees. Submit the withholding tax certificate (Sor Sor 3) to the organiser as proof of payment.

foreign.label: Foreign Companies
foreign.value: International exhibitors are not subject to Thai withholding tax. 7% VAT still applies to all service fees settled in Thailand.

receipt.label: Receipts & Tax Invoices
receipt.value: Official receipts are issued for all payments. Full tax invoices (ใบกำกับภาษีเต็มรูปแบบ) are issued within 5 business days of receipt.

schedule.title: When Payments Are Due
schedule.subtitle: Late payments may result in booth cancellation or service suspension

deposit.label: 50% Deposit — Upon Signing
deposit.value: 50% of total booth fee is due within 15 days of signing the booth contract. Booth assignment is held until deposit is received.

second.label: Balance — September 15, 2026
second.value: Remaining 50% of booth fees plus all confirmed service orders are due by September 15, 2026.

services.label: Service Orders — October 10, 2026
services.value: All additional service orders (electricity, furniture, AV, WiFi) must be settled by October 10, 2026.

onsite.label: On-site Orders
onsite.value: Orders placed during build-up are payable on-site. Cash or bank transfer only. On-site orders carry a 20% surcharge.

late.label: Late Payment Fee
late.value: Invoices unpaid after the due date are subject to a 1.5% per month late fee. The organiser reserves the right to suspend services for delinquent accounts.

contact.title: Finance & Billing Team
contact.subtitle: For invoice queries, payment confirmations, and billing corrections

billing.label: Billing Enquiries
billing.value: billing@gcatgs.com — Include your exhibitor ID and invoice number in all correspondence.

emergency.label: On-site Billing (Oct 15–19)
emergency.value: Billing office at QSNCC Hall Office, Ground Floor. Open 08:00–18:00 during build-up and show days.

form.label: Form B01
form.value: Download Form B01 — Billing Information Form from the portal. Submit before August 1, 2026 for timely invoice processing.
